Continuous Improvement Manager
Michael Page Procurement & Supply Chain
FeaturedEasy Apply
Continuous Improvement Manager
1 week ago by Michael Page Procurement & Supply Chain
Michael Page Procurement & Supply Chain jobs
£60,000 - £70,000 per annum
Stockport, Cheshire
Permanent, full-time
Continuous Improvement Manager role based in Stockport. Working for w well established, market leading manufacturing company.

Client Details

Our client is a well established, growing manufacturing company who are a market leader in their field.

Description

· Lead change initiatives across all areas of the business by identifying, quantifying, and managing projects from operations, manufacturing, supply chain, and support functions.

· Develop the Continuous Improvement (CI) strategy and deploy lean tools and techniques to meet business requirements, focusing on process improvements in Safety, quality, cost, service, and inventory management whilst growing the people within.

· Implement best practice lean tools and techniques within the business, including Kaizen / Lean Six Sigma, by coaching and training managers, supervisors, and staff; lead Kaizen and 5S events to ensure methodologies align with the business and customer demands.

· Achieve annualized project savings as outlined in the business strategy/growth plans; coordinate project activities during profit planning and support the Operations team with project management and departmental goals.

· Continuously enhance project management techniques and coach others in their use, ensuring clear management and timely closure of projects, with accurate plans and concise, high-quality status presentations.

· Support and reduce business issues through root cause analysis and corrective action plans for all areas of the business.

· Engage the entire organization to promote Lean/CI benefits and foster a culture of continuous improvement as a core business ethos.

· Maintain a flexible approach to CI, adapting to the changing needs of the business to achieve goals and objectives inline with the full business strategy agreed at senior level.

· Demonstrate proficiency in process improvement tools such as Value Stream Mapping (VSM), Standard Work, and 5S; coordinate self-assessments and implement action plans to address gaps.

· Develop and advance tools and methodologies as appropriate to meet business requirements.

· Deliver projects and plans that achieve annualized savings, increase OEE as defined by business objectives

· Lead, train, and coach others in lean tools and techniques, building expertise throughout the business and fostering a culture of continuous improvement to meet all business KPIs.

· Own and manage the Kaizen calendar, ensuring deployment and support of activities throughout the business.

· Support Value Analysis/Value Engineering (VAVE) events and activities to achieve targeted cost reductions where appropriate.

Profile

· You will be a proven process / continuous improvement manager within a manufacturing environment.

· You may have come from a production, operations or quality background within manufacturing originally but will now be an established continuous improvement manager

· Relevant proven experience and or six sigma trained (demonstrated)

· Demonstrated experience within a manufacturing/warehouse and or engineering environment

· Motivated to deliver/achieve with a engaging approach and take people with them on the journey

· Ability to work with all levels of the business and set clear pathways vs strategy

· Demonstrates a collaborative approach

· Experience in working with and training cross-functional groups

· IT literate - proficient in SAP use of SharePoint and able to bring new ideas technology into the business

Job Offer

£60,000 to £70,000 plus bonus and benefits

salary: £60,000 - £70,000 per annum plus bonus and benefits
shortCompanyReason: Well established, market leading manufacturing company
shortRoleReason: Lead continuous improvement initiatives to enhance processes and achieve savings
companySummary: A well established, growing manufacturing company and market leader in their field.
roleSummary: Lead change initiatives and deploy lean tools across operations, manufacturing, supply chain, and support functions to improve safety, quality, cost, service, and inventory management while developing people.
headlineAttraction: Lead continuous improvement in a market leading manufacturing company with competitive salary and benefits
rolePurpose: To lead and manage continuous improvement initiatives across the business to enhance processes, reduce costs, and foster a culture of continuous improvement.
advertSummary: Permanent full-time Continuous Improvement Manager role based in Stockport with a market leading manufacturing company, focusing on lean methodologies, project management, and continuous improvement to deliver business savings and growth.
personalisedIntro: I bring hands-on experience leading continuous improvement initiatives in manufacturing and operations settings, focusing on lean methodologies that enhance safety, quality, and cost efficiency. This role aligns with my background in deploying structured change and coaching teams to embed sustainable improvements.
whyThisRole: The role’s emphasis on leading lean deployment across multiple functions matches my experience managing cross-functional projects and driving operational discipline. I’m motivated by the challenge of embedding continuous improvement culture while delivering measurable savings and efficiency gains.
coreResponsibilities: Lead change initiatives across operations, manufacturing, supply chain, and support functions
Develop and deploy continuous improvement strategy using lean tools
Implement lean tools including Kaizen, Lean Six Sigma, 5S
Coach and train managers, supervisors, and staff in lean methodologies
Achieve annualized project savings aligned with business strategy
Coordinate project activities and support operations with project management
Conduct root cause analysis and corrective action plans
Promote Lean/CI benefits and culture across the organization
Maintain flexibility to adapt CI approach to business needs
Use process improvement tools like Value Stream Mapping and Standard Work
Manage Kaizen calendar and support related activities
essentialRequirements: Proven continuous improvement management experience in manufacturing
Experience in production, operations or quality within manufacturing
Six Sigma training or equivalent demonstrated experience
Experience in manufacturing, warehouse or engineering environment
Ability to engage and motivate people at all business levels
IT literate with proficiency in SAP and SharePoint

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Driving lean transformation and operational resilience in manufacturing environments
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I bring hands-on experience leading continuous improvement initiatives in manufacturing and operations settings, focusing on lean methodologies that enhance safety, quality, and cost efficiency. This role aligns with my background in deploying structured change and coaching teams to embed sustainable improvements.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: This company stands out as a market leader in manufacturing with a clear growth trajectory, which offers a solid platform to apply and expand my continuous improvement expertise. The established yet evolving environment is appealing for delivering impactful operational enhancements.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: The role’s emphasis on leading lean deployment across multiple functions matches my experience managing cross-functional projects and driving operational discipline. I’m motivated by the challenge of embedding continuous improvement culture while delivering measurable savings and efficiency gains.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Leading continuous improvement initiatives in manufacturing
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I led day-to-day operations during a manufacturing scale-up, improving delivery reliability and operational resilience, which directly relates to driving lean initiatives and process discipline here.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Hero metric and supporting point for manufacturing operations leadership
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I strengthened operational performance and delivery discipline during a manufacturing growth phase.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Achieving cost savings and financial discipline
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I supported tighter financial and operational controls that improved profit and working capital, demonstrating my ability to deliver cost savings aligned with business strategy.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Supporting point for cost reduction and financial impact
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I contributed to profit improvement and released working capital through operational controls.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Complex annual projects and team leadership
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I managed complex projects and led a team in a high-pressure environment, which shows my capability to coach and engage teams effectively in continuous improvement activities.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Supporting point for leadership and coaching skills
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I led and supported teams delivering complex projects while maintaining service standards.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: This role requires someone who can lead and embed lean continuous improvement across manufacturing and support functions, delivering cost savings and operational efficiencies while developing people. It needs a practical leader who can adapt CI approaches to evolving business needs and engage teams at all levels.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Lead continuous improvement initiatives across manufacturing and operations
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I managed operations during a manufacturing scale-up, improving delivery discipline and resilience.
experienceMappings.relevance: Direct experience leading operational improvements in manufacturing environments.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Achieve cost savings aligned with business strategy
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I supported tighter financial and operational controls that improved profit and working capital.
experienceMappings.relevance: Proven ability to deliver financial benefits through operational improvements.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Coach and train managers, supervisors, and staff in lean methodologies
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I led and supported teams through complex projects, maintaining standards and developing capability.
experienceMappings.relevance: Experience in team leadership and coaching under pressure.

focusAreasToBring.title: Lean Deployment Across Functions
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on embedding lean tools like Kaizen and 5S across operations, manufacturing, and supply chain, tailoring approaches to meet specific team needs.

focusAreasToBring.title: Cost and Operational Efficiency
focusAreasToBring.summary: I aim to identify and deliver targeted cost savings and operational improvements that align with strategic goals and enhance overall business performance.

focusAreasToBring.title: People Development and Engagement
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would prioritize coaching and training to build continuous improvement capabilities and foster a collaborative culture throughout the organisation.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: My background in managing manufacturing operations and leading continuous improvement projects fits well with this role’s requirements. I bring a balance of operational discipline, financial awareness, and people leadership that supports sustainable lean transformation.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: In the first year, I would expect to deliver measurable savings through lean project deployment, improve process reliability, and strengthen team capabilities in continuous improvement. I would also focus on embedding a culture that embraces ongoing operational excellence.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in collaborative, engaging environments where continuous improvement is valued. I’m comfortable working across teams and levels to build consensus and drive change.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would review current CI initiatives, meet key stakeholders, and assess operational challenges and opportunities.
I would develop targeted lean deployment plans, initiate quick-win projects, and begin coaching key managers and supervisors.
I would support wider roll-out of CI activities, track savings delivery, and refine training programs to build sustainable capability.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I’m ready to lead continuous improvement efforts that deliver real operational and financial benefits while building a strong lean culture.
closingProofPoints: Led manufacturing operations through scale-up, improving delivery and resilience.
Supported profit and working capital improvements through tighter operational controls.
Managed complex projects and coached teams to maintain high standards.
contentNotes: The advert provides strong detail on role expectations and culture, enabling focused content.
Evidence examples are well matched to manufacturing operations and continuous improvement needs.
Company highlights are limited to market leadership and growth; no further culture details available.
First 90 days plan is based on typical CI manager onboarding and role priorities.
